  Manages a Data Protection (DLP) enforcement policy: what traffic it targets (MCP servers or model providers), which detection engines evaluate it, the action taken on a finding, and which principals it applies to.

# barndoor_dlp_enforcement_policy (Resource)

Manages a Data Protection (DLP) enforcement policy: what traffic it targets (MCP servers or model providers), which detection engines evaluate it, the action taken on a finding, and which principals it applies to.

A policy targets exactly one lane, and the API rejects mixed shapes: `MCP_SERVER` policies use `mcp_targets` (with `RUNTIME_STAGE_TOOL_INPUT`/`RUNTIME_STAGE_TOOL_OUTPUT` stages) and may not set `provider_ids`/`model_alias`; `MODEL_PROVIDER` policies are the reverse (with `RUNTIME_STAGE_PROMPT`/`RUNTIME_STAGE_RESPONSE` stages). When `target_kind` is unset the API infers it from the scope attributes.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Tokenize PII found in tool traffic to every MCP server, for members of the
# "engineering" group. target_kind is inferred (MCP_SERVER here); priority is
# assigned by the API when unset.
resource "barndoor_dlp_enforcement_policy" "mcp_pii" {
  name   = "Tokenize PII on MCP traffic"
  action = "POLICY_ACTION_TOKENIZE"

  mcp_targets = [
    { mcp_server_id = "*", direction = "BOTH" },
  ]

  principals = [
    { principal_type = "GROUP", principal_id = "engineering" },
  ]

  # Detection engines that evaluate the traffic (UUIDs of engines configured
  # in the organization).
  detection_engine_ids = ["00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000"]
}

# Block findings in prompts sent to a specific model provider.
resource "barndoor_dlp_enforcement_policy" "llm_prompts" {
  name          = "Block secrets in prompts"
  action        = "POLICY_ACTION_BLOCK"
  provider_ids  = ["openai"]
  runtime_stage = "RUNTIME_STAGE_PROMPT"

  # Roll out observing first; flip to false to enforce.
  dry_run = true

  detection_engine_ids = ["00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000"]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `action` (String) Action taken when a detection engine reports a finding: `POLICY_ACTION_TOKENIZE`, `POLICY_ACTION_REDACT`, `POLICY_ACTION_PASSTHROUGH`, `POLICY_ACTION_ALERT_ONLY`, `POLICY_ACTION_BLOCK`, `POLICY_ACTION_MASK`, or `POLICY_ACTION_OMIT`. Every referenced detection engine must support the action (the API rejects unsupported combinations with a 422).
- `detection_engine_ids` (List of String) IDs (UUIDs) of the detection engines that evaluate traffic for this policy, in evaluation order. At least one is required, and each engine must belong to the organization and support the policy's `action`.
- `name` (String) Policy name. Must be unique among the organization's enforcement policies.

### Optional

- `dry_run` (Boolean) Whether the policy only observes (records findings without acting). Defaults to `false`.
- `mcp_targets` (Attributes Set) MCP servers the policy targets (`MCP_SERVER` policies only). Use `mcp_server_id = "*"` to target every server — the wildcard may not be mixed with specific server IDs. Omit to match every server as well. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--mcp_targets))
- `model_alias` (String) Model alias the policy is scoped to (`MODEL_PROVIDER` policies only). Omit to match every model.
- `principals` (Attributes Set) Principals (groups or roles) the policy is limited to. Omit to apply to everyone. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--principals))
- `priority` (Number) Evaluation priority — lower wins, and the value is unique per organization and target kind. Assigned by the API (next free value in the target kind's lane) when unset.
- `provider_ids` (List of String) Model provider IDs the policy is scoped to (`MODEL_PROVIDER` policies only). Omit to match every provider.
- `runtime_stage` (String) Runtime stage the policy applies to: `RUNTIME_STAGE_PROMPT` / `RUNTIME_STAGE_RESPONSE` (`MODEL_PROVIDER`), `RUNTIME_STAGE_TOOL_INPUT` / `RUNTIME_STAGE_TOOL_OUTPUT` (`MCP_SERVER`), or `RUNTIME_STAGE_UNSPECIFIED` (every stage of the target lane — the server default).
- `target_kind` (String) Which lane the policy targets: `MCP_SERVER` or `MODEL_PROVIDER`. Inferred by the API when unset (`MODEL_PROVIDER` when `provider_ids`, `model_alias`, or a prompt/response `runtime_stage` is present; `MCP_SERVER` otherwise).

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (String) When the policy was created (RFC 3339).
- `created_by` (String) Subject that created the policy (empty when the platform could not attribute it).
- `id` (String) Policy UUID assigned by the API; also the `terraform import` key.
- `org_id` (String) Organization the policy belongs to, resolved from the provider credential's token claims.
- `updated_at` (String) When the policy was last updated (RFC 3339).
- `updated_by` (String) Subject that last updated the policy.

<a id="nestedatt--mcp_targets"></a>
### Nested Schema for `mcp_targets`

Required:

- `direction` (String) Traffic direction the target covers: `REQUEST`, `RESPONSE`, or `BOTH`. Must be compatible with the policy's `runtime_stage` (`REQUEST` for tool input, `RESPONSE` for tool output).
- `mcp_server_id` (String) MCP server the target names, or `*` for every server.


<a id="nestedatt--principals"></a>
### Nested Schema for `principals`

Required:

- `principal_id` (String) Group or role the policy is limited to.
- `principal_type` (String) Kind of principal: `GROUP` or `ROLE`.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import by the enforcement policy's UUID (the resource's id).
terraform import barndoor_dlp_enforcement_policy.mcp_pii 11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111
```
